heading: The Only White That

pillars.heading: Geological Rarity
pillars.body: Makrana marble is classified among the world's rarest calcitic marbles. Its dense crystalline calcite structure is what geologists attribute to its exceptional resistance to discolouration over centuries.

pillars.heading: Proven Permanence
pillars.body: Used in UNESCO World Heritage construction — including the Taj Mahal — for over 400 years. Its endurance across centuries is a matter of documented geological and architectural record.

pillars.heading: Absolute Singularity
pillars.body: Each block carries unique veining formed over 500 million years. No two sculptures share the same interior light. Every piece is, by nature, unrepeatable.

quote: The same quarry. The same mineral. The same ancient light.
